sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with right knee pain, which began 5 days ago after twisting the knee while water skiing on Lake Martin.
The patient reports that the knee swelled and has been sore since the injury.
The patient denies hearing or feeling anything pop during the incident and has not had any numbness or tingling in the foot.
The patient has taken over-the-counter Aleve twice a day and applied a cold pack to the knee, which has provided some relief.
The patient has never had any similar injuries to this knee before.
The patient has a history of high blood pressure, which is currently being managed with lisinopril 20mg daily, and has been taking it as directed.
The patient's blood pressure has been around 125/80 most days at home.
Past medical history: The patient has had a tonsillectomy in the past.
The patient's husband makes them watch baseball, but they are not a fan themselves.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77 today, which is slightly lower than their reported home readings of 125/80.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion in the right knee.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient has decreased range of motion, a negative varus and valgus test, a negative Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain right knee; Hypertension; Past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for the medial collateral ligament strain to help reduce swelling and pain.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I also ordered a lipid panel to check the patient's lipid levels.
I continued the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension and administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the results of the x-ray and lipid panel are available.
